model.name: AI-BOK Reference Architecture
model.documentation:
AI-BOK Reference Architecture ============================
This ArchiMate model is the architecture translation of the AI Body of Knowledge (AI-BOK) v1.0, written by Jan Willem van Veen (ArchiXL).
The model provides a complete reference framework for AI governance and architecture, structured according to the ArchiMate 3.2 metamodel across all layers:
Elements: 668 Relations: 1056 Views: 48
Content per layer: Motivation: 76 principles, 16 requirements, 14 goals, 41 standards, 7 stakeholders, 10 drivers, 52 AI concepts (SKOS) Strategy: 12 capabilities, 5 value stream stages, 6 maturity approaches Business: 12 functions, 29 roles, 17 processes, 7 quality gates, 28 business objects, 88 actors/vendors Application: 23 ABBs, 16 services, 10 meta-model objects Technology: 109 SBBs (concrete products and frameworks) Impl/Migr: 4 roadmap phases, 3 gaps, 5 maturity levels
Core architecture: the Cognition Plane as an independent architecture layer alongside the Control Plane and Data Plane, connected via services.
Three foundations: Semantic Precision, Reliable Knowledge Sources, Explicit Governance.
Views are organised in 6 folders: 01 Overview — Navigator, Capability Map, Layered View 02 Business - Roles, Lifecycle, Value Stream, RACI, Processes 03 Application — Three Planes, ABBs, SBBs, Application Cooperation 04 Motivation - Stakeholders, Principles, Requirements, Standards 05 Maturity - Maturity, Quality, Roadmap 06 Knowledge Areas - Detail per KA (KG1-KG12)
Source references: each element has a dct:source property with page reference to the AI-BOK v1.0 publication.
Author: Jan Willem van Veen / ArchiXL Licence: Open source, free to use as reference Website: https://www.ai-bok.nl Version: 1.0 (April 2026)

element.name: KA1: AI Governance
element.documentation: Create conditions under which AI systems deliver maximum value within acceptable risk boundaries.

element.name: KA2: AI Strategy & Portfolio Management
element.documentation: Maximum organisational value from AI investments through systematic selection and prioritisation.

element.name: KA3: AI Architecture
element.documentation: A coherent, repeatable and evolvable blueprint for AI capabilities.

element.name: KA4: AI Lifecycle Management
element.documentation: Predictable, repeatable and governable progress of AI initiatives.

element.name: KA5: Data & Semantics for AI
element.documentation: Reliable, traceable and semantically anchored data foundation for AI.

element.name: KA6: Model Management
element.documentation: Reliable, reproducible and responsible model portfolio.

element.name: KA7: AI Interaction & User Experience
element.documentation: AI interactions that are user-centred, reliable and inclusive.

element.name: KA8: AI Operations
element.documentation: Reliable, observable and cost-efficient AI operations in production.

element.name: KA9: AI Risk Management & Safety
element.documentation: Proactively identify, assess and mitigate AI-related risks.

element.name: KA10: AI Compliance & Audit
element.documentation: Verifiable, risk-based compliance of AI systems.

element.name: KA11: AI Ethics & Responsible AI
element.documentation: Ethical considerations structurally embedded in the design and deployment of AI.

element.name: KA12: AI Knowledge & Context Management
element.documentation: AI systems produce grounded, traceable and semantically vamemberated output.

element.name: Idee & Kans
element.documentation: Identify AI opportunities and assess business value.

element.name: Design
element.documentation: Architecture, data preparation and model selection.

element.name: Development
element.documentation: Training, fine-tuning, prompt engineering and evaluation.

element.name: Production
element.documentation: Deployment, integration, monitoring and operations.

element.name: Value
element.documentation: Measurable business value and continuous improvement.

element.name: AI Governance & Steering
element.documentation: Establishing and enforcing AI policy, principles and mandates.

element.name: AI Strategy & Portfolio Management
element.documentation: Selection, prioritisation and monitoring of AI initiatives.

element.documentation: Designing and maintaining the AI reference architecture.

element.name: AI Lifecycle Management
element.documentation: Guiding AI initiatives through all phases of the lifecycle.

element.name: AI Data Management & Semantics

element.name: AI Interaction Management
element.documentation: Design and optimisation of AI user interactions.

element.name: AI Operations Management
element.documentation: Deployment, monitoring and operational reliability.

element.name: ArchiXL
element.documentation: Dutch architecture firm, specialist in enterprise and information architecture. Creator of WikiXL, ArchiMedes and BegrippenXL.

element.name: Chief AI Officer (CAIO)
element.documentation: Ultimately responsible for AI strategy, governance and value creation. Chair of the AI Board and guardian of AI-BOK implementation.

element.name: AI Portfolio Manager
element.documentation: Manages the AI portfolio: selection, prioritisation and monitoring of AI initiatives based on business value and strategic fit.

element.name: AI Ethics Advisor
element.documentation: Advises on ethical aspects of AI systems: fairness, bias, transparency and societal impact. Member of the AI Ethics Committee.

element.name: AI Product Owner
element.documentation: Responsible for functional steering of an AI initiative: requirements, prioritisation and acceptance from the business perspective.

element.name: AI Architect
element.documentation: Designs the AI reference architecture: planes, ABBs, integration patterns and technology choices. Ensures coherence and reusability.

element.name: AI Risk Manager
element.documentation: Identifies, assesses and mitigates AI-related risks. Manages the AI risk register and advises on risk classification (EU AI Act).

element.name: AI Governance Lead
element.documentation: Operationalises AI governance: policy frameworks, mandates, quality gates and compliance processes. Member of the AI Review Committee.

element.name: AI Knowledge Manager
element.documentation: Curates and manages knowledge sources for AI grounding: terminology frameworks, knowledge graphs and document collections.

element.name: AI Engineer
element.documentation: Develops, trains and optimises AI models: data engineering, model training, fine-tuning, prompt engineering and evaluation.

element.name: AI Interaction Designer
element.documentation: Designs user interaction with AI systems: conversation flows, explainability, feedback mechanisms and human-in-the-loop patterns.

element.name: AI Operations Engineer
element.documentation: Responsible for deployment, monitoring and operational management of AI systems in production. Monitors SLAs and performance.

element.name: AI Quality Analyst
element.documentation: Evaluates and vamemberates AI systems for quality: faithfulness, relevance, bias, robustness and conformity to quality gates.

element.name: AI Auditor
element.documentation: Performs independent audits on AI systems: compliance, technical conformity, data quality and governance adherence.

element.name: AI System Administrator
element.documentation: Manages the technical AI infrastructure: platforms, API gateways, model registries, monitoring tools and access control.

element.name: AI Trainer/Annotator
element.documentation: Prepares training data: labelling, annotation, quality control and vamemberation of datasets for supervised learning.

element.name: AI Domain Expert
element.documentation: Provides domain knowledge for AI systems: output vamemberation, curation of knowledge sources and assessment of domain-specific quality.

element.name: AI Change Manager
element.documentation: Guides organisational change in AI adoption: stakeholder management, training, communication and culture change.

element.name: AI Agent Governor
element.documentation: Manages autonomous AI agents: defines mandates, escalation rules, autonomy boundaries and monitors agent behaviour in production.

element.name: AI Compliance Officer
element.documentation: Ensures compliance with AI legislation: EU AI Act, GDPR, sector-specific requirements and reporting obligations.

element.name: Knowledge Engineer
element.documentation: Designs and manages knowledge graphs and ontologies for AI grounding.

element.name: RAG Architect
element.documentation: Designs and optimises RAG pipelines and retrieval strategies.

element.name: AI Fairness Engineer
element.documentation: Specialist in bias-detectie, fairness-metrics en debiasing-technieken.

element.name: Explainability Specialist
element.documentation: Designt en implementeert uitlegbaarheidsmechanismen (SHAP, LIME).

element.name: AI Security Architect
element.documentation: Beveiligingsarchitectuur voor AI-systemen, prompt injection preventie.

element.name: AI Platform Engineer
element.documentation: Builds and manages the AI platform (MLOps, serving, monitoring).

element.name: Content Curator
element.documentation: Selects and qualifies knowledge sources for RAG pipelines.

element.name: Semantic Specialist
element.documentation: Expert in terminologieraamwerken, SKOS, linked data voor AI.

element.name: AI Solution Architect
element.documentation: Designt concrete AI-oplossingen binnen de referentiearchitectuur.

element.name: Data Protection Officer
element.documentation: Data Protection Officer, oversight of GDPR compliance in AI.

element.name: Ideation & Exploration
element.documentation: Identification of AI opportunities, feasibility analysis and alignment with business strategy. Deliverable: validated AI business case.

element.name: Design & Architecture
element.documentation: Architecture design, model selection, data preparation and integration patterns. Deliverable: approved architecture blueprint.

element.name: Data Acquisition & Preparation
element.documentation: Collection, cleaning, labelling and validation of training and evaluation data. Deliverable: qualified datasets.

element.name: Model Development & Training
element.documentation: Training, fine-tuning, prompt engineering and experimentation. Deliverable: trained and validated model with model card.

element.name: Evaluation & Validation
element.documentation: Quality evaluation on faithfulness, bias, robustness and conformity to quality gates. Deliverable: evaluation report and go/no-go.

element.name: Deployment & Integration
element.documentation: Deployment to production, integration with business processes and user acceptance. Deliverable: operational AI system.

element.name: Monitoring & Operations
element.documentation: Continuous monitoring of performance, drift, costs and anomalies. Triggers retraining loop on degradation. Deliverable: operational logs and alerts.

element.name: Evolution & Decommissioning
element.documentation: Evaluation of continued use, knowledge transfer and responsible decommissioning. Deliverable: lessons learned and decommissioning report.

element.name: TOGAF Integration
element.documentation: AI Architecture as 5th layer (Cognition Plane). AI Architect on Enterprise Architecture Board.

element.name: DAMA-DMBOK Integration
element.documentation: KA5/KA12 extend data governance. Terminology frameworks in metadata management.

element.name: COBIT/ITIL Integration
element.documentation: AI systems in CMDB. AI incidents via existing incident management + AI classification.

element.name: COSO/ISO 31000 Integration
element.documentation: AI risk register integrated in enterprise risk register. Three Lines of Defence with AI-specific controls.

element.name: GDPR Integration
element.documentation: DPIA for AI systems. Art. 22 right to explanation operationalised. Privacy by design.

element.name: Agile/SAFe Integration
element.documentation: Gate criteria as Definition of Done per PI. MLOps pipeline as CD Pipeline equivalent.

element.name: Retraining Loop
element.documentation: P7 → P4: Performance degradation triggers retraining.

element.name: Evaluation Loop
element.documentation: P5 → P4: Gezakte evaluatie keert terug naar modelontwikkeling.

element.name: Evolution Loop
element.documentation: P8 → P1: Fundamental changes restart the full cycle.

element.name: Data Bias Assessment
element.documentation: Assessment per dataset on bias and representativeness.

element.name: Supply Chain Risk Assessment
element.documentation: Risk assessment of external models, datasets, services.

element.name: Audit Trail
element.documentation: Immutable log of AI decisions and interactions.

element.name: LLM/Foundation Model Service
element.documentation: Inference endpoint for language models. Provides text, image and code generation via foundation models.

element.name: Agent Orchestrator
element.documentation: Orchestration of autonomous AI agents, task decomposition, tool use and multi-agent coordination.

property.value: High (agent has access to business data)

element.name: RAG Pipeline
element.documentation: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: retrieves relevant context from knowledge sources and feeds it to the LLM.

element.name: Prompt Registry
element.documentation: Central management of prompt templates, system messages, version control and effectiveness metrics.

element.name: Guardrails Engine
element.documentation: Input/output filtering, content moderation, prompt injection detection and safety controls.

element.name: Evaluation Framework
element.documentation: Automated quality evaluation of models: faithfulness, relevance, hallucination rate.

element.name: AI Identity & Access Mgmt
element.documentation: Identity and authorisation for AI agents, users and systems. Agent identities (NHI).

element.name: Policy Engine
element.documentation: Policy rules for AI authorisation, policy-as-code. Runtime governance checks for agents.

element.name: Audit Trail Service
element.documentation: Immutable logging of AI decisions, interactions and agent actions. Provenance and traceability.

element.name: Model Registry
element.documentation: Central registry of models, versions, model cards, experiments and deployments.

element.name: API Gateway
element.documentation: Access control, rate limiting, routing and load balancing for AI services and LLM endpoints.

element.name: Vector Database
element.documentation: Storage and similarity search of embeddings for RAG and semantic search.

element.name: Knowledge Graph
element.documentation: Knowledge graph for structured knowledge: entities, relations, ontologies (RDF/OWL/SKOS).

element.name: Feature Store
element.documentation: Standardised storage and serving of ML features for training and inference.

element.name: Data Lake/Warehouse
element.documentation: Central storage for training data, evaluation data and analytics.

element.name: Document Store
element.documentation: Document storage for RAG sources: policy documents, manuals, knowledge articles.

element.name: Monitoring & Observability
element.documentation: Continuous monitoring of AI systems: performance, costs, drift, anomalies and SLA monitoring.

element.name: Bias Detection & Fairness
element.documentation: Detection and mitigation of bias in models and data. Fairness metrics and debiasing techniques.

element.name: Explainability Engine
element.documentation: Uitlegbaarheidsmechanismen voor AI-beslissingen: feature attribution, counterfactuals, attention maps.

element.name: Data Quality Framework
element.documentation: Geautomatiseerde datakwaliteitsVamemberation: volledigheid, consistentie, actualiteit, biasdetectie.

element.name: Annotation Tooling
element.documentation: Tooling for data annotation, labelling and curation. Support for RLHF and domain expert validation.

element.name: CI/CD & Infrastructure
element.documentation: Continuous integration/delivery pipelines, containerisatie, infrastructure-as-code voor AI-workloads.

element.name: Model Serving
element.documentation: Production-serving van ML-modellen: batching, caching, GPU-optimalisatie, A/B-testing.

element.name: Naive RAG
element.documentation: Basis retrieve-then-generate patroon.

element.name: Advanced RAG
element.documentation: Pre-retrieval optimalisatie, post-retrieval reranking.

element.name: Modular RAG
element.documentation: Modulaire pipeline met verwisselbare componenten.

element.name: GraphRAG
element.documentation: Knowledge Graph-gebaseerde retrieval met entity-relatie context.

element.name: Agentic RAG
element.documentation: Agent-driven retrieval with tool use and iterative search strategies.

element.name: Data Retrieval Service
element.documentation: Vector similarity search and document retrieval for RAG pipelines.

element.name: Knowledge Query Service
element.documentation: SPARQL and graph queries on knowledge graphs and ontologies.

element.name: Data Quality Service
element.documentation: Geautomatiseerde datakwaliteitsVamemberation en -rapportage.

element.name: Feature Serving Service
element.documentation: On-demand serving of ML features for training and inference.

element.name: Identity & Authorization Service
element.documentation: Agent-identiteit, autorisatie en mandaatverificatie.

element.name: Policy Check Service
element.documentation: Runtime governance checks: may this agent perform this action?

element.name: Audit & Logging Service
element.documentation: Immutable logging of decisions, interactions and agent actions.

element.name: Model Management Service
element.documentation: Modelselectie, versiebeheer en model card raadpleging.

element.name: API Access Service
element.documentation: Controlled access to AI endpoints with rate limiting and routing.

element.name: Monitoring & Observability Service
element.documentation: Continuous monitoring of performance, drift, costs and anomalies.

element.name: AI Inference Service
element.documentation: Tekstgeneratie, classificatie, embedding via foundation models.

element.name: Agent Orchestration Service
element.documentation: Multi-agent coördinatie, taakdecompositie en tool-gebruik.

element.name: Knowledge Retrieval Service
element.documentation: RAG: grounded answers based on authoritative knowledge sources.

element.name: Guardrails & Safety Service
element.documentation: Input/output-filtering, Hallucinationpreventie en content moderation.

element.name: AI Evaluation Service
element.documentation: Geautomatiseerde kwaliteitsevaluatie: faithfulness, relevance, fairness.

element.name: Pseudonymisation Service
element.documentation: De-pseudonymisation only possible by authorised roles with specific task key.

element.name: Key Vault Pattern
element.documentation: Pseudonymisation layer separating personal data from content data. Task keys link pseudonymised data to tasks, not to persons.

element.name: EU AI Act
element.documentation: European regulation for AI with risk classes.

element.name: Digital Transformation
element.documentation: Organisations must leverage AI to remain competitive.

element.name: Trust in AI
element.documentation: Public trust in AI is essential for adoption.

element.name: Rise of Agentic AI
element.documentation: Autonomous AI agents require new governance mechanisms.

element.name: Technological Acceleration
element.documentation: Rapid development of foundation models and agentic AI.

element.name: Talent Scarcity
element.documentation: Labour market for AI specialists is very tight.

element.name: Organisational Culture & Change Readiness
element.documentation: Culture determines AI adoption pace.

element.name: Sector-specific Regulation
element.documentation: Additional requirements per sector (healthcare, finance, government).

element.name: Sustainability & ESG (CSRD)
element.documentation: AI energy consumption and CO2 emissions are reported.

element.name: Data Spaces & Supply Chain Digitisation
element.documentation: Federated data sharing requires interoperable AI.

element.name: KA1: Responsible AI Governance
element.documentation: Create conditions under which AI systems deliver maximum value within acceptable risk boundaries.

element.name: KA2: Maximum AI Value Creation
element.documentation: Maximum organisational value from AI investments through systematic selection and prioritisation.

element.name: KA3: Coherent AI Architecture
element.documentation: A coherent, repeatable and evolvable blueprint for AI capabilities.

element.name: KA4: Predictable AI Lifecycle
element.documentation: Predictable, repeatable and governable progress of AI initiatives.

element.name: KA5: Reliable Data Foundation
element.documentation: Reliable, traceable and semantically anchored data foundation for AI.

element.name: KA6: Manageable Model Portfolio
element.documentation: Reliable, reproducible and responsible model portfolio.

element.name: KA7: User-Centred AI Interaction
element.documentation: AI interactions that are user-centred, reliable and inclusive.

element.name: KA8: Reliable AI Operations
element.documentation: Reliable, observable and cost-efficient AI operations in production.

element.name: KA9: Proactive Risk Management
element.documentation: Proactively identify, assess and mitigate AI-related risks.

element.name: KA10: Demonstrable AI Compliance
element.documentation: Verifiable, risk-based compliance of AI systems.

element.name: KA11: Ethically Responsible AI
element.documentation: Ethical considerations structurally embedded in the design and deployment of AI.

element.name: KA12: Grounded AI Knowledge Processing
element.documentation: AI systems produce grounded, traceable and semantically vamemberated output.

element.name: Responsible AI Deployment
element.documentation: AI is deployed in a responsible, transparent and safe manner.

element.name: AI Compliance
element.documentation: Full compliance with EU AI Act and relevant standards.

element.name: Semantic Precision
element.documentation: Unambiguous definitions of the concepts AI uses.

element.name: Reliable Knowledge Sources
element.documentation: Authoritative, curated information for grounding.

element.name: Explicit Governance
element.documentation: Clear rules, roles and mandates.

element.name: Responsibility is explicitly assigned
element.documentation: Every AI system has a designated owner responsible for functioning, impact and policy compliance.

element.name: Governance is proportional to risk
element.documentation: Governance intensity is proportional to the risk classification of the AI system.

element.name: Transparency about autonomy
element.documentation: The degree of autonomous operation is documented and visible.

element.name: Governance is embedded, not imposed
element.documentation: Governance mechanisms are part of the AI architecture, not an afterthought.

element.name: Human control as a design principle
element.documentation: Human-in-the-loop or human-on-the-loop is built in for decisions with significant impact.

element.name: Continuous improvement based on metrics
element.documentation: Governance is not static but is periodically adapted.

element.name: Strategic alignment over technological ambition
element.documentation: AI initiatives are selected based on contribution to business objectives.

element.name: Portfolio balance as a steering mechanism
element.documentation: The AI portfolio is deliberately balanced between explorative and exploitative.

element.name: Value realisation over activity
element.documentation: What counts is not the number of AI projects but the realised value.

element.name: Capacity planning as a precondition
element.documentation: AI strategy without capacity planning is a wish list.

element.name: Adaptive planning in a dynamic field
element.documentation: Strategy is reviewed annually; portfolio at least quarterly.

element.name: Cognition plane as a strategic layer
element.documentation: Strategy addresses the cognition plane as a new architecture layer.

element.name: Semantic precision over ambiguity
element.documentation: AI-systemen moeten concepten eenduidig interpreteren via terminologieraamwerken.

element.name: Explicit governance over implicit control
element.documentation: Authorities and escalation rules for AI agents are explicit in the architecture.

element.name: Separation of responsibilities
element.documentation: Architecture separates data, knowledge, logic and governance as four layers.

element.name: Traceability as an architecture requirement
element.documentation: Every AI output must be traceable to sources, models and reasoning steps.

element.name: Interoperability through open standards
element.documentation: AI components communicate via MCP and A2A. Vendor lock-in is minimised.

element.name: Design for evolution
element.documentation: Architecture is modular and loosely coupled so that components are replaceable.

element.name: Privacy by design through pseudonymisation
element.documentation: Personal data is never unnecessarily exposed to AI components.

element.name: Phase-aware governance
element.documentation: Each lifecycle phase has its own quality criteria, roles and deliverables.

element.name: Continuous feedback loops
element.documentation: Production observations structurally flow back to earlier phases.

element.name: Reproducibility as a precondition
element.documentation: Every experiment, training and deployment must be repeatable.

element.name: Value-driven prioritisation
element.documentation: The lifecycle is driven by business value, not just technical feasibility.

element.name: Responsible decommissioning
element.documentation: End of lifecycle is as important as the beginning: audit trail, data retention, knowledge transfer.

element.name: Agentic awareness
element.documentation: For agentic AI, additional lifecycle requirements apply per phase.

element.name: Semantic precision over data volume
element.documentation: Data value for AI is determined by unambiguity, not by volume.

element.name: Grounding in authoritative sources
element.documentation: AI-systemen verankeren antwoorden in traceerbare, autoritatieve kennisbronnen.

element.name: Traceability from source to output
element.documentation: Every AI output is traceable to data sources and semantic definitions.

element.name: Data quality as a continuous process
element.documentation: Data quality is an ongoing process of measurement, detection and improvement.

element.name: Bias awareness in all data phases
element.documentation: Bias detection is built into every phase of the data pipeline.

element.name: Interoperability through open standards
element.documentation: Semantische modellen gebruiken SKOS, RDF, OWL, SHACL.

element.name: Every model is a managed asset
element.documentation: Every production model is registered in a central model registry.

element.name: Documentation as a first requirement
element.documentation: No model to production without a model card.

element.name: Reproducibility is non-negotiable
element.documentation: Every model version must be reproducible.

element.name: Bias testing is structural
element.documentation: Modellen worden periodiek getest op ongewenste biases.

element.name: Deliberate choice: foundation model vs custom model
element.documentation: The choice is an architecture decision with consequences.

element.name: Model portfolio thinking
element.documentation: Models are managed as a portfolio: overlap and risks are visible.

element.name: User first
element.documentation: Every AI interaction is designed from the perspective of task, context and knowledge level.

element.name: Trust calibration
element.documentation: Users form a realistic picture of what AI can and cannot do.

element.name: Transparency in operation
element.documentation: The system proactively communicates about sources and uncertainties.

element.name: Inclusive accessibility
element.documentation: AI interfaces are usable for users with diverse abilities.

element.name: Human autonomy
element.documentation: The user retains control over the decision-making process.

element.name: Iterative improvement
element.documentation: Interactionpatronen worden continu geëvalueerd en verbeterd.

element.name: Production reliability
element.documentation: AI systems function with the same reliability as business-critical applications.

element.name: Observability
element.documentation: Performance, costs, quality and deviations are continuously measured.

element.name: Reproducibility
element.documentation: Every deployment is reproducible: models, configurations and prompts are versioned.

element.name: Automated delivery
element.documentation: CI/CD pipelines for maximum automation from model to production.

element.name: Cost management
element.documentation: AI-inferentiekosten worden actief gemonitord en geoptimaliseerd.

element.name: Gradual rollout
element.documentation: New versions are rolled out in phases (canary, A/B, blue-green).

element.name: Risk proportionality
element.documentation: Risk management intensity is proportional to the risk level.

element.name: Proactive identification
element.documentation: Risico's worden vóór deployment geïdentificeerd en geadresseerd.

element.name: Continuous vigilance
element.documentation: Risk profiles change; monitoring is continuous, not one-off.

element.name: Safety as culture
element.documentation: AI safety is an organisational culture, not a checklist.

element.name: Legal conformity
element.documentation: EU AI Act risk classification is the minimum framework.

element.name: Transparent accountability
element.documentation: Risk assessments are documented and available for audit.

element.name: Demonstrable compliance
element.documentation: Compliance is only real when verifiable and reproducible.

element.name: Risk-based classification
element.documentation: Compliance effort scales with risk level.

element.name: Independence of audit
element.documentation: Audits worden uitgevoerd door functioneel onafhankelijke partijen.

element.name: Continuous traceability
element.documentation: Audittrails worden continu vastgelegd, niet achteraf gereconstrueerd.

element.name: Proactive transparency
element.documentation: Organisaties publiceren actief in het Algorithmregister.

element.name: Integral alignment
element.documentation: AI Compliance integreert met enterprise governance en informatiebeveiliging.

element.name: Fairness as a measurable requirement
element.documentation: Fairness is a testable property with standardised metrics.

element.name: Explainability as a right
element.documentation: Stakeholders have the right to an understandable explanation of AI decisions.

element.name: Human oversight as a fundamental principle
element.documentation: Human-in/on/in-command per AI system based on risk and impact.

element.name: Societal responsibility
element.documentation: Organisaties beoordelen ook bredere maatschappelijke consequenties.

element.name: Value alignment as a design requirement
element.documentation: AI systems are designed in line with organisational and societal values.

element.name: Ethics by design
element.documentation: Ethical considerations are structurally integrated into the design process.

element.name: Grounding over generation
element.documentation: AI output based on verified, authoritative sources.

element.name: Explicit source authority
element.documentation: Knowledge sources are classified by reliability and authority.

element.name: Hallucination prevention as a system requirement
element.documentation: Prevention of factually incorrect output is a hard system requirement.

element.name: Knowledge currency as a continuous process
element.documentation: Mechanisms for staleness detection are structurally embedded.

element.name: Semantic vamemberation
element.documentation: Every knowledge source and AI output is validated against terminology frameworks.

element.name: Federated knowledge sharing
element.documentation: Knowledge is unlocked at the source and shared according to data spaces principles.

element.name: SKOS
element.documentation: W3C standard for terminology frameworks and thesauri.

element.name: NL-SBB (Geonovum)
element.documentation: Standard for Describing Concepts.

element.name: RDF / RDFS
element.documentation: W3C Resource Description Framework for linked data.

element.name: OWL
element.documentation: W3C Web Ontology Language for ontologies.

element.name: SHACL
element.documentation: W3C Shapes Constraint Language for validation.

element.name: SPARQL
element.documentation: W3C query language for RDF data.

element.name: DCAT 3.0
element.documentation: W3C Data Catalog Vocabulary for dataset metadata.

element.name: DCAT-AP-NL
element.documentation: Dutch profile for data catalogue.

element.name: JSON-LD
element.documentation: Linked data serialisation format.

element.name: W3C PROV-O
element.documentation: Provenance ontology for origin registration.

element.name: Dublin Core
element.documentation: Metadata standard for information sources.

element.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023
element.documentation: AI Management System standard.

element.name: NIST AI RMF 1.0
element.documentation: AI Risk Management Framework.

element.name: EU AI Act
element.documentation: European regulation for AI governance.

element.name: AVG / GDPR
element.documentation: European privacy regulation.

element.name: ISO/IEC 25010
element.documentation: Software product quality characteristics.

element.name: IEEE P2247
element.documentation: Adaptive Autonomous Systems Architecture.

element.name: TOGAF
element.documentation: Enterprise Architecture framework.

element.name: ArchiMate 3.2
element.documentation: Architecture modelling language.

element.name: MIM
element.documentation: Information Modelling Metamodel (Geonovum/VNG).

element.name: OpenLineage
element.documentation: Pipeline metadata lineage standard.

element.name: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
element.documentation: Agent-tool integration protocol.

element.name: A2A (Agent-to-Agent)
element.documentation: Agent-agent communication protocol.

element.name: Model Cards (Mitchell et al.)
element.documentation: De facto standard for model documentation.

element.name: Datasheets for Datasets (Gebru)
element.documentation: Standard for dataset documentation.

element.name: Agent Card (A2A)
element.documentation: Agent metadata specification.

element.name: SKILL.md (Agent Skills)
element.documentation: Open standard for AI interaction patterns.

element.name: ISO/IEC 5338:2023
element.documentation: AI Lifecycle Processes.

element.name: ISO/IEC 22989:2022
element.documentation: AI Concepts & Terminology.

element.name: ISO/IEC 23894:2023
element.documentation: AI Risk Management.

element.name: ISO/IEC 25012
element.documentation: Data Quality Model.

element.name: ISO 9241-210:2019
element.documentation: Human-Centred Design.

element.name: WCAG 2.2 / EN 301 549
element.documentation: Accessibility standard.

element.name: FAIR Data Principles
element.documentation: Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable.

element.name: DMN (Decision Model & Notation)
element.documentation: Standard for decision models.

element.name: SBVR
element.documentation: Semantics of Business Vocabulary and Business Rules.

element.name: BPMN 2.0
element.documentation: Business Process Model and Notation.

element.name: ONNX
element.documentation: Open Neural Network Exchange format.

element.name: OpenAPI Specification
element.documentation: REST API specification standard.

element.name: FinOps Framework
element.documentation: Cloud financial management framework.

element.name: ISO 31000:2018
element.documentation: Risk Management standard.

element.name: AI Impact Assessment
element.documentation: Assessment of impact and risks per AI system.

element.name: Level 1: Initial
element.documentation: Ad hoc, no structured AI governance.

element.name: Level 2: Repeatable
element.documentation: Basic processes and roles defined.

element.name: Level 3: Defined
element.documentation: Organisation-wide standards and procedures.

element.name: Level 4: Managed
element.documentation: Quantitatively managed, continuous monitoring.

element.name: Level 5: Optimised
element.documentation: Continuous improvement, innovation-driven.

element.name: Dimension: Governance
element.documentation: Policy, decision structures, responsibilities.

element.name: Dimension: Process
element.documentation: Lifecycle processes, quality gates, feedback loops.

element.name: Dimension: People
element.documentation: Roles, competencies, training, culture.

element.name: Dimension: Technology
element.documentation: Tooling, platforms, infrastructure, automation.

element.name: Dimension: Culture
element.documentation: AI Literacy, innovation mindset, ethical awareness.

element.name: AI Maturity Assessment
element.documentation: Assessment of AI maturity across 5 dimensions and 5 levels.

element.name: FRIA (Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment)
element.documentation: Assessment of impact on fundamental rights.

element.name: DPIA for AI
element.documentation: Data Protection Impact Assessment specifically for AI systems.

element.name: AI Quality Scorecard
element.documentation: Quality score across 5 dimensions per AI system.

element.name: Data Quality
element.documentation: Completeness, currency, representativeness, labelling, bias, semantic consistency.

element.name: Model Quality
element.documentation: Accuracy, robustness, reproducibility, generalisability, fairness.

element.name: Interaction Quality
element.documentation: Task success, user satisfaction, trust calibration, Hallucinationpercentage.

element.name: Operational Quality
element.documentation: Availability, reliability, scalability, drift detection speed, costs.

element.name: Governance Quality
element.documentation: Compliance ratio, auditability, transparency, ethical review coverage.

element.name: Policy-as-code Governance
element.documentation: Governance rules must be machine-readable and runtime-evaluable.

element.name: Immutable Audit Trail
element.documentation: All AI decisions and interactions must be immutably logged.

element.name: Agent Authorisation and Mandate
element.documentation: Agents must be authorised at runtime based on explicit mandates.

element.name: Input/Output Guardrails
element.documentation: AI output must be filtered for safety, correctness and ethics.

element.name: Continuous Observability
element.documentation: AI systems must be continuously monitored for performance, drift and costs.

element.name: Semantic Anchoring
element.documentation: AI systems must interpret concepts via authoritative terminology frameworks.

element.name: End-to-end Traceability
element.documentation: Every AI output must be traceable to sources, models and reasoning steps.

element.name: Central Model Management
element.documentation: All production models must be registered with model cards and versions.

element.name: Structural Bias Detection
element.documentation: Models must be periodically tested for undesirable biases.

element.name: Explainability of decisions
element.documentation: Stakeholders have the right to an understandable explanation of AI decisions.

element.name: Privacy by design
element.documentation: Personal data is never unnecessarily exposed to AI components.

element.name: Grounding in Authoritative Sources
element.documentation: AI output must be based on verified, traceable knowledge sources.

element.name: Open standards and interoperability
element.documentation: AI components communicate via standardised protocols (MCP, A2A).

element.name: Automated Delivery Pipeline
element.documentation: Model-to-production must be maximally automated via CI/CD.

element.name: User-centred Prompt Design
element.documentation: AI interactions are designed from the perspective of task, context and knowledge level.

element.name: Continuous Data Quality Monitoring
element.documentation: Data quality is an ongoing process of measurement and improvement.

element.name: Board of Directors
element.documentation: Strategic decision-making on AI deployment and risk acceptance.

element.name: CIO / CDO
element.documentation: Responsible for IT/data strategy and AI integration.

element.name: Business Owner
element.documentation: Owner of the business process supported by AI.

element.name: End User
element.documentation: Employee who works with AI systems daily.

element.name: Citizen / Customer
element.documentation: Person affected by AI decisions.

element.name: Regulator
element.documentation: External party overseeing AI Compliance (e.g. DPA, ACM).

element.name: AI Team
element.documentation: Multidisciplinary team of AI specialists.

element.name: AI
element.documentation: A technology that, for explicit or implicit purposes, infers how to generate outputs based on received input (such as data). Outputs include predictions, content, recommendations and/or decisions. The technology can learn, reason and perform tasks in a way that normally requires human intelligence.

property.value: AI (Artificial Intelligence) refers to the broader concept of artificial intelligence - the science and technology that enables machines to mimic human cognitive tasks such as learning, reasoning and problem-solving. This includes techniques such as machine learning, neural networks and natural language processing.

AI (artificial intelligence) is a technology where computers perform tasks normally done by humans, such as learning, thinking and problem-solving. AI can recognise faces, understand text or drive a car autonomously. This is done with smart programs that process large amounts of data and learn from them.

element.name: AI-systeem
element.documentation: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment, and that, for explicit or implicit objectives, infers from the input it receives how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations or decisions that can influence physical or virtual environments.

property.value: Examples of AI systems are machine learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP) and computer vision (classifiers).

An AI system encompasses not only the model but also the entire infrastructure around it. This includes hardware, software, data processing, input and output interfaces, and all components needed to make the model work effectively. Examples are ChatGPT, Google Gemini and Co-Pilot.

element.name: Algorithm
element.documentation: A set of rules and instructions that a computer automatically follows when making calculations to solve a problem or answer a question.

property.value: By algorithm we mean a set of rules and instructions that a computer executes, with goals such as solving problems, answering questions, executing tasks or making decisions.
A recipe for solving a computer science problem from a given initial state to an intended end goal, consisting of a finite sequence of unambiguously defined instructions or rules.

element.name: Model
element.documentation: A program trained to recognise patterns in data and make predictions.

property.value: For example, predicting weather, recognising cats.
A model is a simplified representation of something from the real world. It helps to better understand, investigate or predict something.

element.name: AI-model
element.documentation: An AI model is the result of training an algorithm on data. An algorithm is a set of instructions, and the model is the specific result of following that set of instructions based on certain data.

property.value: A model is trained on a task, for example classifying documents. GPT-4 is an example of an AI model.

element.name: Machine Learning
element.documentation: The way computers learn new things without explicit programming, by learning from labelled data to make predictions.

property.value: Self-learning algorithms are algorithms that train themselves. This process is called machine learning. This allows computers to learn from data without explicit programming. This is a very common form of AI.

element.name: Supervised Learning
element.documentation: Machine learning process where a model learns from labelled examples (input-output pairs), comparable to a teacher-student scenario.

property.value: Your algorithm learns from data you label with information. For example, you offer photos with labels: this is a cat, this is not a cat.

element.name: Unsupervised Learning
element.documentation: Machine learning proces waarbij AI zelf verborgen patronen in ongelabelde data zoekt zonder expliciete begeleiding.

property.value: You let the algorithm discover patterns and structures itself in unstructured data without labels.

element.name: Reinforcement Learning
element.documentation: The algorithm learns through punishment and reward. The goal is to score as high as possible in as little time as possible.

property.value: For example, you give points when the algorithm sorts photos that look like cats. In reinforcement learning, the AI model learns autonomously. You can also choose to deploy the model frozen.

element.name: Vamemberation
element.documentation: Step in machine learning to verify model performance using a separate dataset (validation set) not used for training.

element.name: Neural Networks
element.documentation: An advanced form of AI that recognises complex patterns in data via layered neural networks, useful for image recognition, speech processing and language understanding.

element.name: CNN (Convolutional)
element.documentation: Vooral gebruikt bij beeldherkenning.

element.name: RNN (Recurrent)
element.documentation: Suitable for sequence data such as text and speech.

element.name: Transformers
element.documentation: Backbone of modern NLP models such as GPT and BERT.

element.name: Generative AI
element.documentation: A form of artificial intelligence that can produce (generate) content (text, images and/or varied content such as music) based on the data it is trained on.

property.value: An example is generating synthetic data and providing programming assistance.
Generative AI, often used by large language models such as GPT-4o and DALL-E 3, focuses on creating new content based on patterns learned from enormous datasets.

element.name: Large Language Model
element.documentation: A specialised type of generative AI model trained on large volumes of text to understand existing content and generate textual content.

property.value: Type of AI trained on large amounts of text data, capable of creating human-sounding text, answering questions, etc.

element.name: GPT
element.documentation: A type of neural network, pre-trained on large text data, that generates clear and relevant text based on prompts.

element.name: RAG
element.documentation: A technique within artificial intelligence that combines the power of generative AI with information retrieval. This means an AI model not only generates text based on pre-trained data, but can also retrieve relevant information from external knowledge sources to provide more accurate and current answers.

element.name: Prompt Engineering
element.documentation: The process of carefully choosing and adapting the input (prompt) for a machine learning model to get the best possible output.

property.value: Examples of prompt engineering: asking clear questions, adding specific instructions for style, creating creative prompts for interesting ideas or designs.
Prompt engineering is the art of smartly formulating instructions for an AI system. By using the right words and structure, an AI can give better and more accurate answers.

element.name: Token
element.documentation: The basic unit of text (a word or part of a word) processed by LLMs.

element.name: Temperature
element.documentation: The degree of randomness in the output of an LLM.

element.name: Hallucination
element.documentation: Output from generative AI that is factually incorrect or does not match reality, despite appearing semantically correct.

element.name: Bias
element.documentation: In the context of AI, bias refers to the assumptions an AI system makes to simplify the learning process and task execution. AI researchers try to minimise bias, as it can lead to poor results or unexpected outcomes.

property.value: Bias in the context of AI refers to systematic errors or prejudices in an algorithm arising from biased training data. This can lead to unfair or inaccurate results. Bias can take various forms: Sample Bias, Measurement Bias, Algorithmic Bias.

element.name: Natural Language Processing
element.documentation: The way computers learn new things without explicit programming, by learning from labelled data to make predictions.

element.name: Named Entity Recognition
element.documentation: Identifying names, locations and concepts in text.

element.name: Sentiment Analysis
element.documentation: Interpreting whether a text is positive, negative or neutral.

element.name: AI-agent
element.documentation: An AI agent is a software entity that autonomously executes tasks and makes decisions based on observations, rules and machine learning algorithms.

property.value: AI agents can vary in complexity, from simple bots to advanced self-learning systems.
An AI agent is a computer program that can independently make decisions and take actions to achieve a goal. It uses AI to gather information, learn and adapt to changes.

element.name: Agentic AI
element.documentation: Agentic AI gives the AI system the ability to act autonomously, for example to create files.

property.value: An example of Agentic AI is a digital assistant that not only answers questions but also independently plans a trip, books tickets and reserves a hotel based on preferences.

Agentic AI refers to artificial intelligence that can independently make decisions and take actions without direct human intervention. This type of AI goes beyond traditional AI systems. Agentic AI can plan, execute and adapt based on changing circumstances.

element.name: Autonomous Agent
element.documentation: These can independently execute complex tasks, such as self-driving cars that analyse traffic conditions and act without direct human control.

element.name: Reactive Agent
element.documentation: These react directly to input without long-term memory. For example, a chess computer that only analyses the current move.

element.name: Multi-agent System
element.documentation: These are multiple AI agents collaborating, for example robots in a factory jointly optimising a production process.

element.name: EU AI Act
element.documentation: The European AI Act is one of the world's first comprehensive laws specifically for artificial intelligence (AI). The AI Act establishes frameworks and requirements for both governments and businesses regarding the development and use of AI systems.

property.value: The purpose of the law is that AI systems used by organisations within the EU are safe and respect fundamental rights. AI systems are divided into different risk categories with corresponding rules.
The AI Act is a European law that sets rules for the development and use of AI systems. The law also grants rights to citizens who come into contact with AI systems.

element.name: AI Literacy
element.documentation: Skills, knowledge and understanding enabling providers, deployers and affected persons, taking into account their respective rights and obligations under the AI Act, to make informed use of AI systems and to become more aware of the opportunities and risks of AI and the potential harm it can cause.

element.name: AI Detector
element.documentation: An AI Detector is a tool designed to detect when a piece of text (or sometimes an image or video) has been created by AI tools (such as ChatGPT and DALL-E). These detectors are not 100% reliable, but they can provide an indication of the likelihood that a text was generated by AI.

property.value: AI Detectors work with similar large language models (LLMs) as the tools they try to detect. They look for low levels of perplexity and burstiness. AI Detectors can be used by universities and other institutions to identify AI-generated content.

element.name: Deepfake
element.documentation: AI-generated or manipulated image, audio or video material that resembles existing persons, objects, places, entities or events, and would be wrongly perceived by a person as authentic or truthful.

element.name: Algorithm Impact Assessment
element.documentation: An Algorithm Impact Assessment is a tool for making trade-off decisions when deploying algorithms and artificial intelligence.

property.value: Examples of Algorithm Impact Assessments are the IAMA and the AIIA.

element.name: Algorithm Register
element.documentation: A register with descriptions of algorithmic applications that directly or indirectly have a societal and/or economic effect on citizens or society as a whole.

element.name: Cognition Plane
element.documentation: The architecture layer where AI systems reason, interpret and make decisions. Distinguished from the data and control planes by non-deterministic, context-dependent behaviour.

property.value: The cognition plane is the core architecture innovation of the AI-BOK. It requires its own governance mechanisms such as mandates and reasoning boundaries.

element.name: Grounding
element.documentation: Anchoring AI output in verified, authoritative sources so that answers are factually correct and traceable.

property.value: Grounding is one of the three foundations of the cognition plane. Without grounding, an LLM produces answers based on training data that may be outdated or incorrect.

element.name: Semantic Precision
element.documentation: Unambiguous, formally defined concepts that enable AI systems to interpret concepts correctly.

property.value: First foundation of the cognition plane. Requires authoritative terminology frameworks (SKOS/NL-SBB).

element.name: AI Governance
element.documentation: The entirety of policy frameworks, decision structures, responsibilities and mandates with which an organisation governs AI systems.

property.value: KA1 is the central knowledge area of the AI-BOK. AI Governance is embedded, not imposed - mechanisms are part of the architecture.

element.name: Shadow AI
element.documentation: Unapproved use of AI tools and services by employees outside the view of IT and governance.

property.value: Shadow AI poses a growing risk. The AI-BOK recommends channeling (providing safe alternatives) over prohibiting.

element.name: Drift Detection
element.documentation: Monitoring shifts in data or model behaviour that degrade the performance of an AI system in production.

property.value: Distinction between data drift (input distribution changes) and concept drift (input-output relationship changes). Trigger for the retraining loop.

element.name: Guardrail
element.documentation: Programmable boundaries on the input and output of AI models that prevent undesirable behaviour.

property.value: Guardrails are architecturally anchored in the cognition plane. They form an additional security layer on top of model training.

element.name: Agent Mandate
element.documentation: Formal specification of the autonomy boundaries, escalation rules and context restrictions within which an AI agent may operate.

property.value: Agent mandates are the governance instrument for agentic AI. They specify what an agent may do, may not do, and when a human must intervene.

element.name: Quality Gate
element.documentation: Formal decision point in the AI lifecycle where an AI initiative is assessed against quality criteria before proceeding to the next phase.

property.value: Each quality gate has specific criteria, responsible roles and deliverables. Comparable to TOGAF ADM quality reviews.

element.name: Model Card (begrip)
element.documentation: Standardised documentation format describing what an AI model does, for whom, with what limitations and what performance.

property.value: Based on Mitchell et al. (2019). Each model card contains: purpose, owner, training data, evaluation results, limitations, fairness assessment.

element.name: Human-in-the-loop
element.documentation: Design pattern where a human is actively involved in the decision-making process of an AI system and assesses every output before it is executed.

property.value: Three variants: human-in-the-loop (every decision), human-on-the-loop (sampling), human-in-command (policy/strategy).

element.name: Knowledge Graph
element.documentation: A graph structure representing entities and their interrelationships as a knowledge base for AI systems.

property.value: Knowledge graphs form a core component of the data plane. They are used for GraphRAG, entity resolution and semantic search functions.

element.name: Embedding
element.documentation: A numerical vector representation of text, image or other data that captures semantic meaning in a continuous vector space.

property.value: Embeddings are the foundation of RAG pipelines and semantic search. Embedding quality determines retrieval effectiveness.

element.name: Fine-tuning
element.documentation: Further training a foundation model on domain-specific data to improve performance for a specific task.

property.value: Alternative to RAG when domain knowledge must be structurally incorporated into the model. More expensive and complex than prompting.

element.name: Responsible AI
element.documentation: The practice of designing, developing and deploying AI systems in a manner that is ethical, transparent, fair and responsible.

property.value: Responsible AI encompasses fairness, explainability, privacy, safety and human oversight. It is the overarching theme of KA11.

element.name: Responsible AI Value Creation
element.documentation: Measurable business value from AI systems within acceptable risk boundaries.

element.name: Level 1: AI as Tool
element.documentation: AI supports individual tasks, no autonomy. User initiates and controls fully.

element.name: Level 2: AI in Workflows
element.documentation: AI is integrated into business processes, automated steps with human supervision.

element.name: Level 3: Digital Assistant
element.documentation: AI conducts conversations, answers questions, assists with decisions. Limited autonomy.

element.name: Level 4: Orchestration
element.documentation: Multiple AI agents collaborate, coordinated by an orchestrator. High governance intensity.

element.name: Level 5: Autonomous Agents
element.documentation: AI agents act independently within mandates. Very high governance requirements.

element.name: Level 6: Federated Collaboration
element.documentation: Agents from different organisations collaborate. Maximum governance complexity.

element.name: Phase 1: Quick Wins (0-3 months)
element.documentation: Inventory existing AI initiatives, assemble AI Board, classify risk, create awareness.

element.name: Phase 2: Foundation (3-12 months)
element.documentation: Governance framework operational, lifecycle established, first models in registry, basic monitoring.

element.name: Phase 3: Scale-up (12-24 months)
element.documentation: Organisation-wide rollout, cognition plane operational, multi-agent pilots, full compliance.

element.name: Phase 4: Optimisation (24+ months)
element.documentation: Continuous improvement, federated collaboration, AI-driven innovation, maturity level 4-5.

element.name: Gap: Geen → Quick Wins
element.documentation: Difference between current situation and first quick wins.

element.name: Gap: Quick Wins → Fundament
element.documentation: Difference between ad-hoc and structured.

element.name: Gap: Fundament → Opschaling
element.documentation: Difference between basic and organisation-wide rollout.
Semantic Precision + Reliable Knowledge Sources + Explicit Governance zijn alle drie nodig.

element.name: Cognition Plane
element.documentation: Reasoning, interpretation, decision-making by AI.

element.name: Control Plane
element.documentation: Identity, authorisation, logging, audit.

element.name: Data Plane
element.documentation: Data, knowledge, vector stores, knowledge graphs.

element.name: 1st Line: Execution
element.documentation: AI Engineers, AI Operations Engineers — dagelijkse operatie en eerste kwaliteitscontrole.

element.name: 2nd Line: Oversight
element.documentation: AI Risk Manager, AI Compliance Officer — onafhankelijke controle en risicobewaking.

element.name: 3rd Line: Assurance
element.documentation: AI Auditor - independent audit of governance and systems.

element.name: Cognition Plane
element.documentation: Vendors in the Cognition Plane.

element.name: Control Plane
element.documentation: Vendors in the Control Plane.

element.name: Data Plane
element.documentation: Vendors in the Data Plane.
